ogTitle: Lower Peacock vs Lower Southgate: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Lower Peacock and Lower Southgate.
heading: Lower Peacock vs Lower Southgate — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Lower Peacock, New South Wales and Lower Southgate, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Lower Peacock has a lower overall climate risk score (39/100) compared to Lower Southgate (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Lower Peacock or Lower Southgate safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Lower Peacock has a flood risk score of 33/100, while Lower Southgate scores 49/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Lower Peacock scores 53/100 for bushfire risk. Lower Southgate scores 47/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Lower Peacock or Lower Southgate?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Lower Peacock has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.52% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
